scenario: Zephyr Bank reviews 3000 transactions per day in a Camunda 8.8 fraud process. Each user task links a Camunda Form by formId on zeebe:formDefinition. The team must pin the form to the exact version deployed alongside the current process so that a later deployment of a newer form does not silently change live process instances.
question: Which bindingType value on zeebe:formDefinition pins the user task to the form version deployed with the current process?

options.text: Setting bindingType to latest on zeebe:formDefinition pins the linked Camunda Form to the version deployed together with the currently running process model
Setting bindingType to versionTag on zeebe:formDefinition pins the linked Camunda Form to the version deployed together with the currently running process model
Setting bindingType to deployment on zeebe:formDefinition pins the linked Camunda Form to the version deployed together with the currently running process model
Setting bindingType to externalReference on zeebe:formDefinition pins the linked Camunda Form to the version deployed together with the currently running process model

a.text: Incorrect. The latest binding resolves to the newest deployed version of the form at the moment the user task activates, which means a later deployment of a newer form does change live instances.

b.text: Incorrect. The versionTag binding selects the latest deployed form annotated with the configured versionTag attribute; it does not key the binding off the process deployment itself.

c.text: Correct. The deployment binding type resolves the linked Camunda Form to the version deployed together with the currently running version of the process, keeping live instances on that exact form version.

d.text: Incorrect. externalReference is not a bindingType value; it is the attribute used to reference a custom form that is not a Camunda Form managed by formId.

explanation: The deployment bindingType on zeebe:formDefinition pins a linked Camunda Form to the version deployed alongside the current process, isolating running instances from later form redeployments.
